title: Malaysian Deaf Sports History
subtitle: A journey through the milestones of deaf sports in Malaysia

events.title: Formation of Malaysian Deaf Sports Association
events.description: The Malaysian Deaf Sports Association (MSDeaf) was established to promote and develop sports among the deaf community in Malaysia, laying the foundation for organized deaf sports in the country.

events.title: First National Deaf Sports Championship
events.description: Malaysia hosted its first National Deaf Sports Championship, bringing together deaf athletes from across the country to compete in athletics, swimming, and team sports.

events.title: Malaysia Joins the World Federation of the Deaf
events.description: Malaysia officially became a member of the World Federation of the Deaf (WFD), opening doors for Malaysian deaf athletes to participate in international competitions.
events.imageAlt: Malaysia joins World Federation of the Deaf

events.title: First Malaysian Deaflympics Participation
events.description: Malaysian deaf athletes made their debut at the Deaflympics (formerly known as the World Games for the Deaf), representing Malaysia on the world stage for the first time.

events.title: Badminton Team Wins ASEAN Deaf Games Gold
events.description: The Malaysian deaf badminton team clinched gold at the ASEAN Deaf Games, marking a historic achievement and putting Malaysian deaf sports on the regional map.
events.imageAlt: Malaysia Deaf Badminton Team wins Gold at ASEAN Deaf Games 1985

events.title: Hosting of 1st ASEAN Deaf Games
events.description: Malaysia proudly hosted the inaugural ASEAN Deaf Games in Kuala Lumpur, welcoming deaf athletes from across Southeast Asia and showcasing the nation's commitment to deaf sports.
events.imageAlt: 1st ASEAN Deaf Games hosted in Malaysia 1991

events.title: Malaysian Deaf Futsal Team Established
events.description: The Malaysian Deaf Futsal Team was officially formed, expanding the range of sports under MSDeaf and creating new opportunities for deaf athletes to compete nationally and internationally.
events.imageAlt: Malaysian Deaf Futsal Team established in 1997

events.title: National Deaf Sports Complex Inaugurated
events.description: A dedicated sports facility for the deaf community was inaugurated, providing a permanent training ground and venue for national deaf sports events.
events.imageAlt: National Deaf Sports Complex inaugurated in 2001

events.title: Historic Deaflympics Medal
events.description: Malaysian athletes brought home medals from the Deaflympics, a landmark achievement that raised the profile of deaf sports in Malaysia and inspired a new generation of deaf athletes.
events.imageAlt: Historic Deaflympics medal for Malaysia in 2005

events.title: Youth Deaf Sports Programme Launched
events.description: MSDeaf launched a nationwide Youth Deaf Sports Programme to identify and nurture young deaf talent, strengthening the pipeline for future national deaf athletes.
events.imageAlt: Youth Deaf Sports Programme launched in 2010

events.title: MSDeaf Golden Jubilee Celebration
events.description: The Malaysian Deaf Sports Association celebrated its 50th anniversary with a grand ceremony, commemorating five decades of promoting deaf sports and honouring the contributions of athletes, coaches, and volunteers.

events.title: Malaysia Hosts Asia Pacific Deaf Games
events.description: Malaysia hosted the Asia Pacific Deaf Games, bringing together deaf athletes from across the Asia Pacific region and reaffirming Malaysia's role as a leader in deaf sports development.
events.imageAlt: Malaysia hosts Asia Pacific Deaf Games 2017

events.title: Virtual Deaf Sports Championships
events.description: In response to global challenges, MSDeaf organised virtual deaf sports championships, embracing technology to keep the deaf sports community connected and competitive.

events.title: New Generation of Deaf Champions
events.description: A new generation of Malaysian deaf athletes emerged, breaking national records and qualifying for international competitions, carrying forward the proud legacy of Malaysian deaf sports.
events.imageAlt: New generation of Malaysian Deaf Champions 2024
